subject: Ecology
title: r and K Strategies
description: A quick, practical guide to r and K Strategies.

hook.text: Some species breed fast and die young. Others breed slowly and invest in each offspring.

buildup.text: r-strategists—like mosquitoes—produce huge numbers and hope that some survive.

discovery.text: K-strategists—like elephants—have few young but protect them for years.

twist.text: Neither strategy is better. Each fits a different ecological pressure perfectly.

climax.text: Weedy fields favor r-strategists; stable old forests favor K-strategists.

punchline.text: Many cheap offspring or few costly ones.

quiz.question: What defines a K-strategist?
options: Few offspring with high parental investment
Many offspring with no parental care
